Definition: Actions that send, publish, or transmit information to external parties.
Example Actions: Send customer email; post externally; submit external form; message vendor or partner.
Recommended Baseline: Treat as high-impact when external, sensitive, irreversible, or business-binding.
Suggested Evidence: recipient; content class or digest; purpose; approval; result

Definition: Actions that access, retrieve, summarize, copy, export, or transmit sensitive data.
Example Actions: Export customer records; retrieve personal data; summarize confidential documents; send internal data to external tools.
Recommended Baseline: Treat export or cross-boundary transfer as high-impact by default.
Suggested Evidence: data class; resource; purpose; source trust; authorization; evidence location

Definition: Actions that initiate, approve, prepare, modify, or execute financial transactions or commitments.
Example Actions: Create purchase order; initiate payment; approve refund; modify invoice or billing record.
Recommended Baseline: Treat monetary commitment above defined threshold as high-impact by default.
Suggested Evidence: amount; currency; counterparty; authority limit; approval; transaction reference

Definition: Actions that grant, revoke, modify, or recommend access rights, roles, credentials, or security-sensitive entitlements.
Example Actions: Change IAM role; grant SaaS admin access; create service account; rotate or retrieve secret.
Recommended Baseline: Treat privilege changes and secret access as high-impact by default.
Key AAEF Control Areas: ID, PRN, DEL, AUZ, RES, EVD
Suggested Evidence: principal; target identity; permission change; policy; approval; revocation path

Definition: Actions that modify production systems, operational configuration, infrastructure, routing, availability, or business-critical workflows.
Example Actions: Change production config; update database state; modify firewall or routing; restart critical services.
Recommended Baseline: Treat production state-changing operations as high-impact by default.
Key AAEF Control Areas: GOV, AUZ, TOOL, HUM, RES, EVD
Suggested Evidence: system; environment; change reference; approval; result; rollback or isolation reference

Category Name: Code Commit, Merge, Deployment, or Execution
Definition: Actions that write, modify, merge, deploy, execute, or trigger code.
Example Actions: Commit code; merge PR; trigger deployment; execute generated script; change CI/CD config.
Recommended Baseline: Treat production deployment and code execution as high-impact by default.
Suggested Evidence: repository; branch; code digest; command; environment; approval; result

Definition: Actions that create, modify, accept, submit, or communicate legal, regulatory, contractual, or compliance-relevant commitments.
Example Actions: Accept contract terms; submit regulatory response; send legal notice; make compliance attestation.
Recommended Baseline: Treat externally binding legal or regulatory actions as high-impact by default.
Suggested Evidence: document reference; recipient; authority; approval; timestamp; submission result

Definition: Actions that materially affect a customer, user, patient, employee, applicant, or other individual.
Example Actions: Suspend account; approve or deny service; issue penalty; change customer status.
Recommended Baseline: Treat eligibility, access, suspension, or adverse customer-impacting decisions as high-impact by default.
Suggested Evidence: affected party; policy basis; decision inputs; approval; outcome; review path

Definition: Actions that affect security posture, detection, response, containment, access, blocking, quarantine, or enforcement.
Example Actions: Block IP; disable account; quarantine endpoint; modify detection logic; suppress alert.
Recommended Baseline: Treat broad, irreversible, or production-impacting security actions as high-impact by default.
Suggested Evidence: incident reference; target; detection source; action; result; rollback path

Category Name: Persistent Memory Write or Trust-State Update
Definition: Actions that write persistent memory, update long-term agent context, modify trust state, or alter future agent behavior.
Example Actions: Write long-term memory; store retrieved content; change trust state; update allowlist or denylist.
Recommended Baseline: Treat persistent memory writes that influence future high-impact actions as high-impact by default.
Suggested Evidence: memory source; provenance; trust level; content digest; expiration; review or deletion path

Category Name: External Agent Delegation or Cross-Domain Agent Action
Definition: Actions that delegate tasks, authority, evidence, tool access, or workflow participation to another agent, especially across trust boundaries.
Example Actions: Delegate to external agent; accept delegated work; invoke external agent; exchange authority claims.
Recommended Baseline: Treat external agent delegation and cross-domain authority exchange as high-impact by default.
Key AAEF Control Areas: ID, PRN, DEL, AUZ, EVD, RES
Suggested Evidence: upstream agent; downstream agent; authority scope; constraints; accepted delegation; revocation status
